title: Incident<->manifest correlation output (TR-03)
description: The schema-versioned, idempotent output of correlate.py. Detection NEVER mutates: the same window + manifest produce byte-identical correlation.json. Each correlated record carries its DERIVED journey_id + step index + affected behavior IDs (derived from the event_name match — telemetry carries no backref; agreement with the audit-produced journeys.json v2 backref is reported in backref_cross_check when --journeys supplies one, ADR 0029), or lands in an explicit no-join bucket with a reason. Class-drift and unmapped-in-prod are surfaced, never dropped; a manifest CORE step absent from the bounded window is informational ONLY (absence-in-a-window is not absence-in-prod — degrade rule 4).

manifest_status.description: Vendored validate_manifest.sh outcome for the input manifest: complete(0)/incomplete(3)/absent. Schema-invalid(4)/unsupported(5) never reach here — TR-03 refuses those (degrade rows §11).

core_steps_absent_in_window.description: Informational ONLY — a manifest CORE step with no emission in the bounded window. NEVER a violation (degrade rule 4).

backref_cross_check.description: The [det-cond] audit-backref agreement check (ADR 0029). Runs when an audit-produced journeys.json v2 (codebase-health CH-02 manifest_journey_id backref) is supplied via --journeys: agreed = every derived journey id backref-confirmed, disagreed names the mismatching ids in the note. Without that artifact (the common prod-triage case) or on a malformed one, status is skipped with the honest reason — never silently assumed.
